core_mechanism: inverse-problem uniqueness for a nonlinear evolution equation — reconstructing an unknown spatially-varying coefficient of the governing law from the asymptotic (large-time / far-field) scattering map that relates incoming to outgoing asymptotic states
brief_reason: The abstract's central claim is that the large-data scattering behavior of the equation uniquely determines the inhomogeneity coefficient a(x), i.e. the asymptotic input-output map inverts to recover a parameter of the governing dynamics.
